/// Interaction logic for IdentifierControl.xaml /// Dependency properties: /// - Party /// - Countries /// - Reasons /// /// The IdentifierControl is used to maintain a list of identifiers /// of different types for a given party. The public interface (bindable dependency properties) /// is as follows: /// /// Party* - any non-abstract entity that inherits from Entities.Party /// Countries* - the list of possible countries as usually supplied by the Entities service /// Reasons* - the list of possible reasons for an identifier not being available /// IdentifierTypes* - a collection of all IdentifierTypes, usually retrieved from Entities service /// IsIdentifierRequired - an identifier must be captured. The initial page allowing a reason for no indentifier /// to be captured will not be displayed if this is set to true. /// DefaultType - a default identifier type may be specified for the first time a new identifer is captured /// FocusOnLoad - set focus to the appropriate control when first loaded /// /// *Mandatory(preconditions) /// /// Notes: /// - valid identifier types will be set up internally based on those existing on the Party.MaintainableIdentifierTypes collection. /// - the mandatory red asterisk will now be part of the control. This will be 'always on' until such time as we have a requirement /// stating otherwise. /// - the control no longer does any filtering of identifier types and countries. /// /// Miscellaneous behaviour: /// - may not add more than 1 identifier of a type for a particular country. /// - may only maintain (add/edit/delete) types in Party.MaintainableIdentifierTypes. This means that other identifiers such as /// Life numbers or HCP numbers may exist, but will be read only. /// - the optional default country is specified as a property (read db column) of IdentifierType, therefore a default country /// property is not necessary. ///
